forward_patch:
--- original +++ modified @@ -0,0 +1,43 @@ +--- +assignees: +- claude-code +position_column: todo +position_ordinal: '9480' +project: ai-panel +title: 'AI panel: merge the collapse control into the AI/model header row' +--- +## What + +The expanded AI panel stacks two header rows, wasting vertical space: + +1. **`AiPanelShell`'s collapse row** (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel-container.tsx`, the `
` block in the expanded branch) — holds nothing but the "Collapse AI panel" button. +2. **`AiPanelHeader`** (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.tsx`) — the real header: the "AI" title on the left, the model selector dropdown on the right (`
`). + +Collapse the two into one. The collapse button belongs on the right of the AI/model-selection header, not in its own row. + +### Approach + +- **Remove** the dedicated collapse-button row from `AiPanelShell`'s expanded branch in `ai-panel-container.tsx`. Keep the absolute-positioned resize handle and the `
{children}
` body. The collapsed-rail branch (`!open`) keeps its own "Expand AI panel" button — do not touch it. +- **Thread the collapse callback into the header.** `AiPanelContainer` already has `handleToggle`; it currently passes it to `AiPanelShell` as `onToggle`. Also pass it into `` as a new `onCollapse` prop. + - Add `onCollapse: () => void` to `AiPanelProps` and forward it to `AiPanelHeader`. + - Add `onCollapse: () => void` to `AiPanelHeaderProps`. +- **Render the collapse button inside `AiPanelHeader`**, to the right of the model selector dropdown (after the ``, still inside the `
`). Reuse the exact control from the old shell row: `aria-label="Collapse AI panel"`, `variant="ghost"`, `size="icon"`, ``. Wrap the selector + collapse button in a flex group so both sit on the right while "AI" stays left. Move the `PanelRightCloseIcon` import from `ai-panel-container.tsx` to `ai-panel.tsx` if it is no longer used by the container. +- The `border-b` separator is preserved — `AiPanelHeader`'s `
` already carries `border-b`. + +Keep the `Container`/`View` split intact: `AiPanelContainer` still owns the open-state; `AiPanelHeader` only renders a button wired to the passed `onCollapse` callback, exactly as it already does with `onSelectModel`. + +## Acceptance Criteria +- [ ] The expanded AI panel shows a single header row: "AI" title left, model selector + collapse button right. +- [ ] The standalone collapse-button row in `AiPanelShell` is gone; the expanded shell renders only the resize handle and the panel body. +- [ ] The collapse button keeps `aria-label="Collapse AI panel"` and the `PanelRightCloseIcon`; clicking it collapses the panel to the rail. +- [ ] The collapsed rail still shows the "Expand AI panel" button and expands on click — unchanged. +- [ ] The `ai.toggle` command still collapses/expands the panel. + +## Tests +- [ ] In `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.test.tsx`: render `AiPanel` with an `onCollapse` spy, assert the header contains the "Collapse AI panel" button, and that clicking it calls `onCollapse`. +- [ ] Confirm the existing `ai-panel-container.test.tsx` collapse/expand test (`"collapses and expands; ..."`, ~line 184 — uses `getByRole("button", { name: /collapse ai panel/i })`) still passes now that the button lives in the header. +- [ ] Update any standalone `AiPanel` render sites in `ai-panel.test.tsx` / `ai-panel.spatial.test.tsx` to pass the new required `onCollapse` prop. +- [ ] Run `cd apps/kanban-app/ui && npx vitest run src/components/ai-panel.test.tsx src/components/ai-panel-container.test.tsx src/components/ai-panel.spatial.test.tsx` — all green. + +## Workflow +- Use `/tdd` — write the header-collapse-button test first, then implement to make it pass.
